tweets.text:
Want to know a secret? 

Leaving my house super early yesterday, I grabbed two shoes and put them on. It was dark and I was hurrying to get to the airport. 

Yesterday afternoon I looked down and realized I had two different colored shoes on!

After two days in NYC, either no one noticed or they couldn't bring themselves to call me out. 👟

tweets.text:
The Morning Minute (7.10)

Powered by @yeet 

⏰Top News:
-Crypto majors rebound 3% across the board; BTC at $64.3k
-Circle receives OCC approval for national trust bank charter; CRCL +10%
-A new draft of the Clarity Act could come next week as clock ticks
-Polymarket seeks regulatory approval to allow margin trading
-Robinhood Chain memes explode as Cashcat jumps 80%, several others up 200%+

🌎 Macro Crypto and Markets
-Crypto majors are green up 2-3%; BTC +3% at $64.3k; ETH +3% at $1,790; SOL +2% at $79; HYPE +3%% at $69
-Morpho (+10%), ARB (+10%) and ZEC (+7%) led top movers
-Oil -2% at $72; Gold even at $4,110
-Stock futures are mixed; DOW +0.2%, Nasdaq -0.2%

-Circle stock is up 8% premarket after the company received OCC approval for a U.S. National Trust Bank charter

-Coinbase Chief Legal Officer Paul Grewal is departing the company

-A new draft of the CLARITY Act could drop as soon as next week, sources say, in a final push for a late-July Senate vote, though it still lacks the bipartisan buy-in needed to pass before the August 7 recess

-New Hampshire’s Executive Council rejected a $100 million Bitcoin-backed bond proposal

-BitGo introduced tools to help institutions reduce quantum computing risk in their Bitcoin holdings

Corporate Treasuries & ETFs
-The Bitcoin ETFs saw $95M in net outflows on Thursday; the ETH ETFs saw $52M in ouflows, breaking their 5-day inflow streak
-Bitcoin miner MARA Holdings agreed to acquire a powered Texas site, sending its stock up 15%

Meme Coin Tracker
-Meme leaders were green; DOGE +3%, SHIB +2%, PEPE +3%, PENGU +3%, TRUMP +1%, BONK +3%
-Robinhood chain memes all leveled up after a huge overnight move; Cashcat +80% to $180M, Juggernaut +110% to $17M, Hoodrat +400% to $15M and Tendies +64% to $6M led top movers
-ANSEM fell another 36% to $200M in the wake of the Robinhood meme move; TripleT +80% and Saylor +170% led Solana movers

💰 Token, Airdrop & Protocol Tracker
-Polymarket is seeking U.S. regulatory approval to allow margin trading, where users could open trading positions without posting the full capital amount
-Goldman Sachs has banned its employees from trading on prediction markets over concerns of inside trading risk
-MoonPay integrated an AI assistant with Telegram for on-the-go crypto trading and analysis, letting users buy, sell, and research directly from chat

🚚 What is happening in NFTs?
-NFT leaders were mixed; Punks even at 32.4 ETH, BAYC -1% at 9.15 ETH, Pudgy +3% at 4.58 ETH; Hypurr’s -2% at 200 HYPE
-Gremlin Cartel (+200%) and Gimboz (+28%) led top movers

Renewed by a makeover (new index, new ticker, fee cut) and fueled by revenge (ever since VOO dethroned SPY), $SPYM is having a year, doubled assets in 12mo to $157b and just secured one of the biggest distribution coups in history as the default ETF in Trump Accounts (the best choice IMO), est $12b/yr in flows to start + future generations are going to grow up $SPYM investors. Do I think it will surpass VOO in flows? Not anytime soon but it could easily be #2 or #3 and IMO will someday be bigger than SPY. Who knows we may start calling it SPYM & Chill. Wrote a note on this today check it out on BI ETF<GO>.

thread_tweets.text: I'm not sure I see Trump Account investors using any of the other options even when they become available.  Hard to see someone wanting to pay more and use IVV and hard to see ppl opting for a broad market ETF (if you look at flows SPX takes in 4-5x what total market ETFs do). So it's not just the default ETF it's the arguably the BEST ETF (altho tbf you'd be well served by any of these options I just don't see ppl opting out of SPYM)

tweets.text:
🇺🇸 #Fed Policy Report Reaffirms Commitment to Price Stability - Bloomberg
- Federal Reserve policymakers will deliver price stability, according to the July Monetary Policy Report to Congress released Friday in Washington.The report noted that “measures of consumer price inflation moved up steadily” in the second half of last year in part due to tariffs, and further this year as energy prices surged in the wake of the Iran war; it also pointed out that some other measures, including the trimmed-mean measure of PCE prices that Fed Chairman Kevin Warsh has highlighted previously, declined over the past year
- The Fed says it is prepared to use its full range of tools to achieve its maximum employment and price stability goals, particularly if the federal funds rate is constrained by its effective lower bound
- The report described labor productivity growth as strong

tweets.text: On this day in 1962, AT&T launched Telstar from Cape Canaveral, the world's first active communications satellite, capable of relaying 600 telephone calls or one television channel simultaneously across the Atlantic. AT&T $T funded the entire $6 million project privately and paid NASA $3 million for the launch, making it one of the first commercial ventures in space. The satellite's first live transatlantic television broadcast, between the U.S. and France, drew an audience of tens of millions and proved that real-time global communication could work as a business. Telstar operated for only seven months before radiation from high-altitude nuclear tests destroyed its transistors. The global satellite communications industry it helped create now generates over $100 billion a year in revenue.

tweets.text:
🇨🇳🇺🇸 #China’s Dollar Deposit Rates Top 3% on Fed Hike Bets - Securities Daily
*Chinese lenders lifted USD time deposit rates above 3% ahead of July Fed meeting, pricing in a potential US rate hike, the Securities Daily reported.Industrial Bank offers 3.2% on one-year lump-sum deposit of $8,000 or more, the report said.
*Lenders including Minsheng Banking Corp. and Bank of Jiangsu launch multi-tenor USD deposit products with entry thresholds as low as $8
*Investors should weigh rate gains against exchange volatility, suggesting diversified USD asset allocation to hedge risks, the newspaper cited Xue Hongyan, an analyst with Jiangsu Sushang Bank Co., as saying.
*Citic Securities Co. economist Ming Ming expects the Fed will more likely hold rates steady this year, and US Treasury yields could decline later amid cooling bets on a hike.
*Link (Chinese): https://t.co/Ty1uciwcrN

tweets.text:
$CRCL extending pre-market gains +13%

Buried in today's OCC approval: Circle's new trust bank enters the custody business, targeting "banks and other financial institutions, such as regulated derivatives organizations" directly.

Interesting to consider the impact on, say, Polymarket, which settles $100B+ annualized in USDC offshore. Onshore it still settles in dollars through FCMs (futures commission merchants). OCC supervision of USDC reserves enhances the case to CFTC and counterparties that USDC is ready as an onshore settlement currency.

Big picture: USDC reserves are moving from state money transmitter oversight to OCC federal supervision. USDC is the first major stablecoin issuer to convert conditional approval to final.
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
$CRCL +11% pre-market, gets OCC nod for digital bank.
This was already baked in the cake with the charter they got last December. Still, they can open for business now. The stock is on its rear-end. Big move confirms how negative sentiment is. https://t.co/tSVnXicHJm

thread_tweets.text:
$CRCL extending pre-market gains +13%

Buried in today's OCC approval: Circle's new trust bank enters the custody business, targeting "banks and other financial institutions, such as regulated derivatives organizations" directly.

Interesting to consider the impact on, say, Polymarket, which settles $100B+ annualized in USDC offshore. Onshore it still settles in dollars through FCMs (futures commission merchants). OCC supervision of USDC reserves enhances the case to CFTC and counterparties that USDC is ready as an onshore settlement currency.

Big picture: USDC reserves are moving from state money transmitter oversight to OCC federal supervision. USDC is the first major stablecoin issuer to convert conditional approval to final.

This was already baked in the cake with the charter they got last December. Still, they can open for business now. The stock is on its rear-end. Big move confirms how negative sentiment is. https://t.co/tSVnXicHJm

tweets.text:
We tackled this question in a recent research report.

See key takeaways: "Many public crypto projects will lose substantial value if they cannot assert their revenue-generating use cases in a world awash in corpchains."

Report in reply. https://t.co/iKbcE20TJr
tweets.quoted_tweet_text:
Honest question-

- Robinhood has its own blockchain

- SWIFT has its own blockchain

- Stripe has its own blockchain

- Wall St has its own blockchain (Canton)

Isn’t it now obvious that real companies doing real things have no interest in existing L1/2s?

I disagree...what is supposed to happen to rental rates over time? Also, the narrative from just a week ago was that meta's sale of capacity reflected a market shifting into over supply. The chart gives multiple dimensions to assess/debunk that line of reasoning.

In general, your tone - for years - is to be a negative/bearish reply guy w/ very little useful contribution to the conversation...so, yeah, I try to be respectful but patience is thin.

tweets.text: Good Morning from Germany, where trade is giving GDP a lift: May exports unexpectedly rose 0.9% MoM, extending gains to a 4th month, while imports fell 2.5%.
